findings.title: EMAIL_PROVIDER_URL env var accepted without scheme validation
findings.why_it_matters: If EMAIL_PROVIDER_URL is set to a non-HTTPS URL (e.g., http://...), credentials and user email addresses are transmitted in cleartext. Edge Function env vars are operator-controlled so exploitation requires operator compromise, making this a defense-in-depth concern.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: `if (providerUrl && !providerUrl.startsWith('https://')) { console.error('[sendEmail] EMAIL_PROVIDER_URL must use HTTPS'); return { sent: false, error: 'Provider URL must use HTTPS' } }`
evidence: No scheme validation — any URL including http:// is accepted

findings.title: pending_deletions logs email in console.error on upsert failure
findings.why_it_matters: The console.error log on pending_deletions upsert failure logs the Supabase error message, but does NOT log the user's email address. However, the email IS visible in the upsert payload in any request-level tracing (APM, function logs with request body). This is acceptable given the non-PII error-only logging pattern, but worth confirming.
evidence: Line 108-112: `console.error('[delete-account] failed to insert pending_deletions row:', { message: pendingError.message })` — only logs error message, not the email

residual_risks: Email API key (EMAIL_API_KEY) is transmitted as a Bearer token in the Authorization header — standard for email providers but worth confirming the provider's API key rotation procedure is documented
testing_gaps: No test that the email send call does not leak PII (email address) into error logs
